title: Advanced Error Handling Patterns
description: Implement advanced error handling patterns: circuit breaker to prevent cascading failures, fallback chains, error boundaries, and multi-validator functions.
starterCode:
# Exercise: Advanced Error Handling Patterns

def circuit_breaker(func, failure_threshold=3):
    """Return a wrapper that tracks consecutive failures.
    After threshold failures, return ("circuit_open", None) without calling func."""
    pass

def fallback_chain(funcs, *args):
    """Try each func in order with *args. Return first success. Raise if all fail."""
    pass

def error_boundary(func, *args, on_error=None):
    """Call func(*args). On error, call on_error(exception) if provided, else return None."""
    pass

def validate_all(validators, value):
    """Run all validator functions on value.
    Return list of error messages from validators that raised ValueError."""
    pass
hints: Use a mutable dict (not simple variables) to track state inside the wrapper closure
circuit_breaker wrapper should raise the exception until the threshold is reached
After threshold failures, the circuit opens and returns ('circuit_open', None) directly
fallback_chain saves each exception and raises the last one if all funcs fail
error_boundary returns on_error(e) if on_error is provided, else None
validate_all runs ALL validators (doesn't stop on first failure) and collects messages

testCases.description: Test circuit_breaker passes through on success
testCases.validation:
cb = circuit_breaker(lambda x: x * 2)
assert cb(5) == 10

testCases.description: Test fallback_chain uses fallback on primary failure
Test error_boundary returns result on success
Test validate_all collects all validation errors
Test circuit_breaker opens after threshold failures
